etymology.description: 激(激) (jī, "stimulate") refers to inciting or provoking energy or emotion; 動(動) (dòng, "move") involves physical or emotional movement or change.
etymology.interpretation: 激(激) (jī, "stimulate") implies provoking strong reactions, often leading to an emotional surge. 動(動) (dòng, "move") signifies transition or change, whether in position or state. Combined, 激動(激動) (jīdòng, "be excited") evokes the image of energy or emotions being stirred up, leading to animated activity or a heightened emotional state.

examples.chinese: 他看到久违的朋友(他看到久違的朋友)，非常激动(非常激動)。
examples.pinyin: Tā kàn dào jiǔwéi de péngyǒu, fēicháng jīdòng.
examples.english: He was very excited to see a long-lost friend.

examples.chinese: 比赛结束时(比賽結束時)，我们都激动得跳了起来(我們都激動得跳了起來)。
examples.pinyin: Bǐsài jiéshù shí, wǒmen dōu jīdòng de tiào le qǐlái.
examples.english: When the game ended, we were all excitedly jumping up.

examples.chinese: 那个激动人心的时刻(那個激動人心的時刻)，他永远难忘(他永遠難忘)。
examples.pinyin: Nàgè jīdòng rénxīn de shíkè, tā yǒngyuǎn nánwàng.
examples.english: That exciting moment, he will never forget.
